mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate has held at 5.25% for four consecutive FOMC meetings after a two-year hiking cycle, signaling the Fed believes policy is sufficiently restrictive. Historically such extended pauses precede equity rallies as markets price in the end of tightening and reduced discount-rate pressure, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS unchanged for 3+ consecutive months after a prior hiking cycle
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following pause onset are not significantly higher than the unconditional base rate (65.5% positive) across a 2-year walk-forward window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year contraction in housing starts signals an accelerating residential investment downturn that historically leads broader demand contractions with a 2-4 quarter lag, dragging down corporate earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage declined from 1772 to 1720 thousand units in latest reading, down sharply from 2005 peaks near 2000+
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns during HOUST YoY declines >15% are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently low and stable unemployment combined with continued nonfarm payroll growth reflects a resilient labor market that sustains consumer income and spending, supporting near-term equity valuations. This always-on trend signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon as labor strength is continuously priced into risk sentiment.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4%, near multi-year lows as of October 2006
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no statistically significant positive skew (t-test p > 0.05) conditional on UNRATE below 4.7% and positive PAYEMS growth, over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are contracting sharply while the federal funds rate remains at cycle-high restrictive levels, the joint condition amplifies financial tightening on the most rate-sensitive sector, increasing the probability of a broader demand-driven slowdown that markets have not yet fully priced, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days beyond what either signal alone would predict.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST declining while FEDFUNDS remains at 5.25%
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy amid housing correction
failure_condition: Joint-trigger 20-day forward returns are not more negative than the single-factor HOUST-only signal by a statistically significant margin (delta Rank IC < 0.02), falsifying the interaction premise.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in industrial production growth below zero on a rolling 3-month basis signals weakening manufacturing activity and an emerging supply-side slowdown that precedes broader earnings disappointments, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 113.1336 with material revision risk noted
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following negative 3-month INDPRO growth are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where CPI inflation is cooling toward the Fed's comfort zone while the funds rate is paused, markets interpret this as a credible soft-landing setup, allowing valuation multiples to expand; this same pause in a regime of accelerating inflation would instead signal policy error risk and depress returns. The mechanism's direction is conditional on the inflation trend accompanying the pause.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS unchanged for 3+ months AND CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change decelerating
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 202.7-202.8 with moderating monthly increases into late 2006
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns under the soft-landing joint condition are not significantly higher than returns under a Fed-pause-with-accelerating-inflation condition (regime split t-test p > 0.05), or overall Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects continued consumer spending strength, the largest component of GDP, which directly supports corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon as the signal is continuously incorporated into market pricing.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value 363673 million dollars, October 2006 reading
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns conditional on positive RSAFS MoM growth show no significant positive skew relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll growth decelerates sharply while the unemployment rate simultaneously begins rising from cycle lows, the joint pattern indicates labor market turning points that historically precede recessions with a multi-month lag, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon that neither series alone reliably signals at low levels.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average monthly gain falls below 100k AND UNRATE rises 0.2pp+ from its trailing 6-month low
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage 135844 thousand, revised to 136843, UNRATE steady at 4.4%
regime_hint: labor market inflection near cycle peak
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns under the joint trigger are not significantly more negative than the PAYEMS-only single-factor signal (delta Rank IC < 0.02), or triggered instances show positive returns in more than 55% of cases.
